faq.a1: Yes, completely free. No hidden fees, no bullshit.
faq.a2: Nobody. Your email is only used to forward messages through our relay system. It's never displayed on the site.
faq.a3: When you report a found item, you receive a one-time identification code. This code is used to mark the item as returned later, ensuring only the original reporter can close the listing.
faq.a4: Yes. Use the code you received when you posted the item to mark it as returned. If you've lost your code or need help closing a listing, email support@fundid.is. For full data deletion, email privacy@fundid.is.
faq.a5: No. Fundið is an independent community project. We are not affiliated with the police or any government agency.
faq.a6: The code system prevents unauthorized closures of listings. All communication goes through the relay, so you can verify the person's story before sharing any details.
faq.heading: FAQ
faq.q5: Is Fundið connected to the police?
faq.q6: What if someone makes a false claim?

how.heading: How does it work?
how.step1: Report a lost or found item. Add a photo, description and drop a pin on the map.
how.step2: Your listing appears on the map and in the item list for others to browse.
how.step3: Someone recognizes the item and sends you a message through Fundið.
how.step4: All communication goes through our email relay, no personal info is shared directly.
how.step5: When an item has been returned the identification code is used to close the listing.

privacy.collected: We temporarily store your email address (to relay messages), item description, photo and where it was lost/found. That's it.
privacy.complaint: You also have the right to file a complaint with Persónuvernd, Iceland's Data Protection Authority.
privacy.email: Your email is never shown publicly, sold, or shared with third parties. No spam, no bullshit.
privacy.heading: Privacy
privacy.intro: Fundið is built on a simple promise: your personal information stays private.
privacy.processors: Your data is stored on Supabase (database) and emails are sent through Resend (email relay). Both operate under standard contractual clauses for data protection.
privacy.relay: All communication between users goes through Fundið's email relay. Neither party sees the other's email address unless they choose to share it.
privacy.retention: Listings and associated data are kept for 90 days after an item is returned and up to 6 months for unclaimed items. After that, they are automatically deleted.
privacy.rights: You have the right to access, correct, or delete your data at any time. Contact us at privacy@fundid.is.

what.body: Fundið is a free, independent platform for lost and found items in Iceland. Anyone can report something they lost or found, and the community helps items find their way home. No fees, no bullshit.
what.heading: What is Fundið?
